page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.large is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core and memory performance. The stress-ng single-core test scored 1890.38, and single-threaded gzip compression reached 28.01 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng performance dropped to a poor rating at 1934.88, reflecting the limited 2-core configuration. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read and write speeds measuring 10.57 GB/sec and 12.34 GB/sec respectively. Passmark CPU and database operations benchmarks also fell into weak or poor categories.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c7t.large offers a dedicated CPU allocation at an entry-level resource tier, making it a budget-conscious option for workloads that require dedicated CPU execution without massive memory or storage overhead. Given the 2 vCPU and 4.0 GB RAM limits, this instance is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, or small-scale development and testing environments rather than memory-intensive databases or complex multi-threaded processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.large is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1890.38 and single-threaded gzip compression at 28.01 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak, with large-block memory reads at 10.57 GB/sec. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level dedicated compute needs. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and small-scale development environments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.large features 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 4.0 GB RAM, offering entry-level compute performance for basic workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core compute instance designed for lightweight single-threaded applications and entry-level development workloads without local storage requirements.
bullet_points: Features 2 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling
Configured without local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic web serving
